deployment.title: Deployment
deployment.description: This page summarizes how to host Iqonga: an optional platform instance (e.g. for development or internal use) and the documentation site at iqonga.org.

development/workflow-enhancements.title: Workflow Enhancements (technical)
development/workflow-enhancements.description: This document reviews the workflow schema and implementation status for: conditional branching, human-in-the-loop, sub-workflows, triggers, router node, observability, and templates.

guides/agent-teams.title: Agent Teams
guides/agent-teams.description: Agent Teams let you group AI agents into named teams. Teams are used when building workflows: each workflow step can be assigned an agent, and organizing agents into teams makes it easier to manage multi-agent flows.

guides/analytics.title: Analytics & Performance
guides/analytics.description: Monitor your agents and content from the dashboard.

guides/company-knowledge.title: Company Knowledge Base
guides/company-knowledge.description: Train your AI agents with your company’s knowledge so they stay on-brand and accurate.

guides/content-generation.title: Content Generation
guides/content-generation.description: Iqonga includes AI-powered content tools you can use from the dashboard and in workflows.

guides/integrations.title: Integrations
guides/integrations.description: Connect your AI agents to channels and tools.

guides/workflows.title: Workflows
guides/workflows.description: Workflows run a sequence of steps. Each step can be an agent (AI does the work), an approval (pause for human approve/reject), a router (branch by condition), or a sub-workflow (call another workflow). Steps run in order unless a router sends the flow elsewhere.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.description: Iqonga is an open-source framework for multi-agent workflows: define sequences of steps, assign AI agents (or human approval, routing, and sub-workflows), and run them manually, on a schedule, or via webhooks.

showcase.title: Showcase
showcase.description: Platforms built with Iqonga. These are live products and communities powered by the open-source framework.
